seoTitle: How to Make YouTube Shorts With Canva (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to create YouTube Shorts in Canva using ready-made templates, quick customization, and export settings built for vertical video.
intro: Canva has become a go-to tool for creators who want polished short-form video without a full editing suite. This guide walks through building a YouTube Short in Canva, from picking a vertical template to exporting a file ready for upload, so you can turn quick ideas into publish-ready content.

steps.heading: Start a Shorts-sized design
steps.body: Open Canva and search for a YouTube Shorts or vertical video template so your canvas is already sized for the 9:16 format Shorts requires.

steps.heading: Pick and customize a template
steps.body: Choose a template that fits your topic, then swap in your own clips, text, and images. Canva's drag-and-drop editor makes it easy to adjust layouts without design experience.

steps.heading: Add branding and text overlays
steps.body: Layer in your logo, brand colors, and captions so the Short is recognizable and easy to follow even with the sound off.

steps.heading: Fine-tune for engagement
steps.body: Trim clips to keep pacing tight, add transitions, and place your hook in the first few seconds since Shorts rely on immediate attention.

steps.heading: Export and upload to YouTube
steps.body: Download the finished video in Canva's recommended format, then upload it directly to YouTube Shorts, double-checking title and description before publishing.

faq.question: Do I need video editing experience to make Shorts in Canva?
faq.answer: No. Canva is built around templates and drag-and-drop tools, so beginners can produce a finished Short without prior editing skills.

faq.question: Can I use Canva's free plan for YouTube Shorts?
faq.answer: Yes, many templates and design tools are available on the free plan, though some premium templates and elements may require a Canva Pro subscription.

faq.question: How do I make sure my Short is the right size for YouTube?
faq.answer: Use a vertical 9:16 template from Canva's Shorts or TikTok/Reels category, since YouTube Shorts is optimized for that aspect ratio.

faq.question: Can Canva help with ideas for the design, not just the layout?
faq.answer: Canva includes AI-assisted suggestions that can recommend design elements matching your brand, which can help when you're not sure where to start.
